Petition To Approve Settlement
Off Calendar Jul-26-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Matt Klinger for (alleged successor trustee) Raymond Fellman. Christopher Lamerdin for the State of California. Deborah Fox for (temporary trustee) Debra Dolch.

The Court stated that there has been a settlement, that a couple of procedural issues have been raised, that the record does not show notice to the Attorney General's nominee and that the Attorney General is not a signatory to the settlement agreement and that it is not sure if these are issues. Mr. Lamerdin stated that they are not issues, that the nominee was a proposed nominee, that they have now agreed on Ms. Dolch and that the request to appoint Mr. Bradlow is withdrawn. The Court asked if there is any reason that the Attorney General needs to be a signatory. Mr. Lamerdin replied that he does not believe so, that as long as Ms. Dolch is acting in her duty they do not need to be a part of the settlement agreement. The Court stated that the final question is that the trustee was not Mr. Anolik but his law firm, and that it raises questions as to whether the bankruptcy created the vacancy. Mr. Lamerdin stated that Mr. Anolik's law firm also declared bankruptcy as well as him and that the Foundation is a creditor. Ms. Fox confirmed that the law firm remains in bankruptcy. The Court granted the Petition to Approve Settlement (subject to receipt/review of the order) requesting that it includes the determination that there is a vacancy in light of the vacancy of trustee. The Court granted the Petition to Appoint Successor Trustee (subject to review of the order). The Petition to Confirm Raymond Fellman as Successor Trustee is off calendar. The Petition for determination of applicability of police or regulatory power exception to bankruptcy stay is off calendar. The petition for removal of trustee and board of directors, etc., is off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For The People Of The State Of Californias Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings To Petition To Confirm Raymond Fellman As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-27-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David DeGroot for and with Raymond Fellman. Christopher Lamerdin for the People of the State of California.

Mr. Lamerdin cited P.C. 15643(f) stating that the bankruptcy creates a vacancy as does a resignation, that the position cannot be vacated twice and that the code is clear on what happens. The Court stated that the petitioners argue that the correct interpretation of the language in the trust is resignation at any time. Mr. Lamerdin stated that it adds facts that are not there and that the vacancy has to mean something. The Court stated that the trust does not mention bankruptcy, that it say at anytime but does not limit it and that if there are two competing interpretations it cannot decide at this time. Mr. Lamerdin stated that if there is a board of directors there must be documentation and that this is a trust not a business. The Court stated that it is not usual that a trustee creates a board of directors but that the trust say that the trustee can. Mr. DeGroot stated that he agrees with the tentative, that he is new to the case, that Mr. Fellman has been involved for 3 years, that if the People's motion is granted he will have spent that time for no reason and cited a previous order by Judge Dearman. The Court stated that Judge Dearman said that no effect would be given to the board's power. Mr. DeGroot stated that it is appropriate to have a final hearing to air the issues once and for all and to make a final ruling. The Court stated that the next hearing is set for Tuesday (2/2/16), that this could be a time to deal with where to go with this and that Mr. DeGroot is to prepare an order denying this motion. Mr. Lamerdin stated that the issues are narrowing, that he thinks they can sit down and talk and that progress is being made.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Sep-01-2015 Continued To Dec-08-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher Lamerdin for the State of California. Raymond Fellman in Propria Persona. Ernest Der for and with (temporary trustee) Debra Dolch.

Mr. Lamerdin reported that Ms. Dolch has now filed a petition to be named permanent successor trustee, that they have provided status, that a number of issues are now moot, that Mr. Anolik can be removed and that Mr. Fellman has no standing. Mr. Fellman stated that he did not know that this would be a hearing and that prior counsel has submitted requests for appointment. The Court stated that it cannot deal with incomplete settlements. Mr. Fellman asked if the matter could go to mediation. Mr. Lamerdin stated that there is nothing to mediate. The Court stated that if Mr. Fellman wants an evidentiary hearing he is entitled to it. Mr. Lamerdin stated that a question of fact is that the previous trustee filed bankruptcy and that the beneficiaries request the appointment of a successor trustee. Mr. Fellman stated that the local rule states that the trustee has a right to elect a successor trustee and that the board of directors elected him. Mr. Lamerdin asked for time to brief the matter prior to the evidentiary hearing. The Court stated that it will give them time to file a motion, that they need to get it all done on the same track, that it will take time to have a motion heard, encouraged Mr. Fellman to get new representation and urged the parties to continue talking. Mr. Der reported that he got the final determination on the appeal yesterday, that the next step is to file a tax court petition, that the decision was negative and that he has consulted with other tax attorneys regarding the issues he is experiencing. The Petition to Appoint Successor trustee, the Petition for removal of trustee and board of directors, the petition for determination of applicability of police or regulatory power exception to bankruptcy automatic stay and the petition to confirm Raymond Fellman as successor trustee were continued December 8, to 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that the continuance is to give them time to get legal work and discovery done and to talk to each other and that it expects on 12/8/15 the issues be teed up for resolution or the parties to present the court with a schedule for resolution.

Off Calendar Jan-08-2014 Continued To Mar-12-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernesst Der for Debra Dolch (Temporary Conservator). Kyong Kim for Ray Fellman (Successor Trustee). Christopher Lamerdin for the Attorney General.

The Court stated that today's hearing is for status of the Foundation. Furthermore, the Court raised an issue with who is taking title from the sale on December 30, 2013, that the sale was confirmed to Edmund Jian in open court but the sale order has Yue Yue LLC on title. Mr. Der explained that Mr. Jian informed him after the sale that the LLC is to hold title, that he is the sole member of the LLC. The Court wants a declaration from Mr. Jian to support his ownership of the LLC.

Mr. Lamerdin reported that Alexander Anolik has not filed an accounting as ordered by the Court. The Court questioned if notice of the order was sent to Mr. Anolik. Mr. Der stated that Mr. Anolik's counsel (Mr. Harryman) was in court and stated that they will do an accounting. Furthermore, Mr. Der stated that he does not know Mr. Anolik's capacity, that he is represented by bankruptcy counsel and that his law practice was sold to his son, Adam Anolik. Ms. Kim stated that Mr. Fellman has no contact with Mr. Anolik, that he has filed a declaration for the two months that he was Successor Trustee. Ms. Kim requested that the petition against Mr. Fellman be dismissed. Mr. Lamerdin argued that dismissal of the petition is premature. Discussions were held with regard to the status of the Foundation. Mr. Der stated that they are trying to file the 2012 income tax but it will take some time to unravel the property expenses and the Foundation expenses. The Petition for Removal of Trustee and Board of Directors, etc., the Petition for Determination of Applicability of Police or Regulatory Power Exception, etc. and the Petition to Confirm Raymond Fell as Successor Trustee were continued to March 12, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status of Mr. Anolik's Accounting is set on the same date. The Court will issue an order to Alexander Anolik to file an accounting for the period from January 1, 2010 to June 30, 2013.

Mr. Der stated that they came up with a proposal that is agreeable to everyone, that they will sell the property held by the Foundation, that they will settle all debts secured by the building, that they will determine what charities the funds will go to and round up the Foundation. Mr. Lamerdin stated that he would like an accounting, that he would like to find out if the foundation has been paying for legal fees for Mr. Anolik and Mr. Fellman, and if the foundation's rights are protected. In addition, Mr. Der stated that they have a Real Estate Agent, that they will provide the listing agreement by way of an ex parte petition, that they will sell the property subject to Court confirmation, that they are currently at $3,000,000.00 on loans secured by the building, that there is an issue of $400,000.00 personal loan that was paid off, that there is a tax issue which they do not have enough information on, and that a forensic accounting will be timely and very costly. The Court ordered that no attorney's fees are to be paid for Mr. Anolik or Mr. Fellman out of any available funds. Mr. Lamerdin requests an accounting for the period of July 25, 2012 through January 10, 2013. The Court suggested Mr. Fellman file a declaration under oath as to what he did or did not do in his capacity. Mr. Harryman stated that his firm is in a position to do an accounting. Off Calendar Mar-06-2013 Continued To Apr-24-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stacie Nelson for and with Raymond Fellman. Christophe Lamerdin representing the Attorney General. David Bradlow ( Proposed Temporary Trustee).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the petition. Ms. Nelson stated that the request to remove Mr. Anolik is now moot since he resigned, that Mr. Fellman has been serving for the past few months and that he can account for the time he served, and that there is a pending Petition to Confirm Mr. Fellman as Successor Trustee on calendar for April 10, 2013. Mr. Lamerdin stated that the matter had been moved to bankruptcy court, that they remanded it back, that Mr. Fellman is not an interested party, and the he should not be petitioning for the appointment. In addition, Mr. Lamerdin presented concerns with regard to case no. CGC-10-505298 (Antonelli & Sons vs. Fellman) and alledged identity theft. Additional argument held. The Court stated that it would make sense to have all the matters heard on April 10, 2013. Mr. Lamerdin presented argument in support of appointing Mr. Bradlow and stated that a temporary trustee should be in place. The Court agreed, stated that a neutral should be in place, and that it would appoint if counsel could agree on someone. Counsel were presented with a list of private professional fiduciaries and were to confer re: the selection of a neutral. The matter was continued to March 11, 2013 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 for status on the selection of a temporary private professional fiduciary. The Petition for Removal continued to April 24, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept 204. At the request of Mr. Lamerdin the Petition to Confirm Raymond Fellman as Successor Trustee currently set for April 10, 2013 is also continued to April 24, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept 204.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Jackie Alameda/ Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.
